_comment: Curated 63-game reference set for v7 GameMap token assignment.
_purpose: Each game represents a distinct hardware driver + technique archetype. The goal is maximum hardware coverage, not general corpus quality. When building GameMapV7, these 63 names are assigned token IDs in order (1-63). Everything else is UNK_GAME.
_status: VERIFIED 2026-05-26 - 63/63 hints verified against corpus GD3 tags via verify_curated_games.py. 10 dashes→colons fixed, 4 corpus-missing games replaced.
_note_on_duplicates: Comix Zone appears twice (SMPS-Derived and GEMS) intentionally - different driver builds of the same game. Gauntlet IV appears twice (Follin and Sakimoto) for the same reason.

driver_groups.notes: Masterclass in clean FM leads, standard DAC sample handling, peak Z80 performance

games.name: Sonic The Hedgehog
games.notes: Masterclass in clean FM leads and standard DAC sample handling

games.name: Sonic The Hedgehog 2

games.notes: Peak Z80 performance with dual-channel sample optimizations

games.notes: Gold standard for Mega Drive club bass emulation

games.notes: Experimental algorithmic industrial techno pushing FM modulating frequencies to limits

games.notes: High-fidelity melodic instrument patches utilizing intense PSG layering

driver_groups.notes: Heavily modified SMPS engines with unique patches and electronic rhythms

games.name: Sonic 3D Blast

driver_groups.driver_name: GEMS (Genesis Editor for Music and Sound)
driver_groups.notes: Sega of America standard tool. Slightly metallic texture due to standard patch library. Essential for the model to differentiate from custom drivers.

games.name: Comix Zone (GEMS Retail)
games.notes: Structural study - compare against custom Drossin variant
games._duplicate_note: Same game as SMPS Custom entry above, different driver build

games.name: Earthworm Jim
games.notes: Tommy Tallarico's bluesy rock maximizing GEMS standard patch set

games.name: Earthworm Jim 2
games.notes: Highly dynamic avant-garde classical adaptations using western tracking styles

games.name: Sonic Spinball
games.notes: Aggressive distorted FM patches highlighting GEMS limitations and unique crunch

games.notes: Slushy heavily modulating industrial sounds from simple waveforms

games.name: Jurassic Park
games.notes: Atmospheric ambient pads and deep rumbling low-frequency sine waves

games.name: Gargoyles

driver_groups.notes: Legendary for the 'heavy metal' sound via raw overdriven FM operators

games.name: Thunder Force III

games.name: Light Crusader

games.name: Flashback

driver_groups.notes: Completely bespoke single-developer or rare proprietary engines squeezing unique textures from the silicon

_implementation_notes.gd3_matching: gd3_name_hint strings are approximate - must verify against actual corpus GD3 game_name_en values before finalizing. Use _normalize_game_name() for comparison.
_implementation_notes.driver_tokens: 10 driver types defined. Consider adding DRIVER_BASE token range to tokenizer_v7 (in the 936-959 reserved range) to provide explicit driver conditioning alongside GAME token.
changes_needed: Add GameMapV7.build_curated(json_path) classmethod that assigns token IDs from curated list order, maps everything else to UNK_GAME
Optionally add DRIVER_BASE token range (10 tokens) to tokenizer_v7 header
Optionally emit DRIVER token in token stream header alongside GAME token
Verify all 63 gd3_name_hint strings match actual corpus GD3 tags (run scan script first)
Decide whether Comix Zone and Gauntlet IV duplicates get same or different token IDs
